presentation.title: Program Coach Deck 01 — Stories That Frame SPIKE

slides.title: Program Coach Deck 01 — Opening
slides.body:
Today we begin with three stories:

1. The AIA Story — why protection matters
2. The Agency Story — how financial entrepreneurs build
3. The SPIKE Story — how you become a venture builder

These stories are not background noise. They are the frame for every deliverable you will create this week.
slides.speakerNotes:
Good morning everyone.
Welcome to SPIKE.
Today is not simply the start of an internship.
It is the beginning of a journey that may shape your career, your business, and perhaps even your life.
Over the next several weeks, you will learn about financial services, entrepreneurship, leadership, teamwork, and personal growth.
But more importantly, you will discover something much more valuable...
You will discover what kind of future you want to build.
SPIKE is not designed to produce interns.
It is designed to develop future financial professionals, entrepreneurs, agency builders, and leaders.
Whether you eventually become an Agency Director...
a specialist financial consultant...
a business owner...
or pursue another path entirely...
our goal is that you leave this program better prepared than when you entered.
Today begins that journey
discussionQuestions: Which story are you most curious to hear today?
What does 'venture builder' mean to you right now — before we define it?

slides.title: The AIA Story
slides.body:
AIA was founded in Shanghai in 1919 with a single conviction: people deserve financial protection when life does not go as planned.

Today, AIA is the largest pan-Asian life insurance group — a company built on trust, discipline, and long-term relationships.

In the Philippines, AIA protects families, funds education, and helps people plan for retirement and legacy. You are not selling products — you are helping people protect what matters most.
slides.speakerNotes:
Before we talk about SPIKE...
I'd like you to understand the organization that made this opportunity possible.
AIA is not simply an insurance company.
We exist for one purpose:
Helping people live healthier, longer, better lives.
Those six words shape every decision we make.
When someone joins AIA, they aren't simply joining a company.
They're joining a mission.
During SPIKE, you'll discover that our conversations with clients are rarely about policies.
They're about protecting dreams...
preparing families...
building healthier futures...
and helping people live with confidence.
That's what we mean when we say:
Healthier. Longer. Better Lives.
discussionQuestions: When have you seen a family struggle because they lacked financial protection?
How does AIA's mission connect to your personal why?

slides.title: The Agency Story
slides.body:
The agency model is not a job — it is a franchise of entrepreneurship.

Financial advisors build books of business. Agency builders recruit, develop, and lead teams. Unit Managers and Agency Directors invest in people the way venture partners invest in startups.

In SPIKE, you learn the Educate → Expand → Empower model: educate your market, expand your client base, empower your team. Your 3-Year Business Blueprint is the agency story written in your own ambition.
slides.speakerNotes:
What do you see?"
People answer:
A family.
Parents.
Children.
Happiness.
Play.
Then ask:
"What don't you see?"
Silence.
Then answer:
You don't see the risks they're protected from.
Powerful.
Think about this...
Every family has dreams.
A first home.
A child's education.
Retirement.
Starting a business.
But every dream faces risk.
Illness.
Accidents.
Disability.
Loss of income.
At AIA, we race against those risks before they become tragedies.
Our work is ultimately about preserving possibilities, not merely paying claims
discussionQuestions: What is the difference between having a job and building a venture?
Who is an agency leader you admire — and what did they build?

slides.title: The SPIKE Story
slides.body:
SPIKE = Startup Business Incubator for Knowledge Entrepreneurs.

You are not interns filling hours — you are venture builders creating:
• Venture Portfolio™ (your personal brand and proof)
• Venture Blueprint™ (your 3-year business plan)
• Financial Entrepreneurship Canvas (your operating model)

Stakeholders:
• You — the venture builder
• Program Coach — program architects and facilitators
• Mentors — your advisory board coaches
• Venture Partners — agency directors who approve milestones
slides.speakerNotes:
"How long does it take to build trust?"
People answer.
Then
"Years."
Pause.
"Sometimes generations."
Pause.
"That's exactly what AIA has spent decades doing.
Trust is earned over time.
AIA Philippines traces its roots back to 1947, when it was established as
Philam
Life.
For generations, Filipino families have trusted the company to help them prepare for life's uncertainties.
Today, AIA Philippines continues that legacy as one of the country's premier life insurance companies, serving millions of individual and group policyholders with a strong financial foundation and a commitment to delivering on its promises.
discussionQuestions: Which output system — Portfolio or Business Plan — feels most unfamiliar?
What milestone at Hour 200 would make you proud?

slides.title: Program Architecture & Squads
slides.body:
Segment 1 (200 hours) → Proof of Concept Venture Board.

Research Squads (3–4): market intelligence and collaborative problem solving.
Advisory Dyads: roleplay, mock client meetings, pitch refinement.
Agency Teams: business planning and franchise simulation.

Week 1 theme: Identity & Purpose — everything today feeds your Venture Portfolio and Blueprint Chapter 1.
slides.speakerNotes:
Many people believe insurance is the product.
It isn't.
The product is peace of mind.
Great financial advisors educate.
They listen.
They ask questions.
They guide families through important decisions.
Insurance simply becomes one of the tools that supports those decisions.
That is why we call this profession financial advising—not selling.
discussionQuestions: What strength will you bring to your research squad?
What do you want from your mentor in Week 1?

slides.title: Deck 01 Close — Transition to Building My Future
slides.body:
You have heard three stories:
• AIA protects what matters
• The agency model rewards builders
• SPIKE turns you into a financial entrepreneur

Next: Program Coach Deck 02 — Building My Future.
You will define Ambition, Impact, Values, Future Self, and Career Direction — the foundation of your Venture Blueprint™.
slides.speakerNotes:
Every Agency Director...
Every MDRT member...
Every successful leader...
Every entrepreneur...
began exactly where you are today.
With curiosity.
None of them started with all the answers.
They learned.
They practiced.
They served.
And eventually, they built careers that changed both their own lives and the lives of others.
SPIKE exists to help you begin that same journey.
Not by telling you what to become...
but by helping you discover what you're capable of building.
discussionQuestions: What is one word that captures how you feel after these three stories?
What question do you want answered about your own future today?

slides.speakerNotes:
Opening (Don't Read the Slide)
Walk toward the audience.
Pause.
Then ask:
How many of you have spent at least 15 years in school?
(Wait for hands.)
Smile.
That's almost everyone.
Continue.
In those 15 years...
you've learned mathematics...
science...
English...
research...
presentations...
examinations...
and many other subjects.
Pause.
Those are all important.
The Question
Then ask:
But let me ask you another question.
Pause.
How many schools actually teach you how to build a business?
Silence.
Don't answer immediately.
Let them think.
Transition
Walk closer.
Speak slowly.
Schools do an excellent job of helping us become employable.
They help us earn degrees.
They help us acquire knowledge.
And we should be grateful for that.
Pause.
But knowledge alone does not automatically create opportunity.
Point to the Slide
Now point to the slide.
Education builds knowledge.
Pause.
Knowledge is powerful.
Pause.
But knowledge is only potential.
Introduce SPIKE
Click.
Reveal:
SPIKE builds entrepreneurs.
Pause.
Don't speak immediately.
Let them read.
Continue.
That's why SPIKE exists.
We are not here to replace your education.
We are here to build on it.
The Bridge
Point to the illustration.
Imagine this bridge.
On one side...
is everything you've learned in school.
Pause.
On the other side...
is what the real world asks of you.
Ask Another Question
When you graduate...
what will employers ask?
Students answer.
Experience.
Skills.
Confidence.
Communication.
Problem solving.
Leadership.
Smile.
Exactly.
None of those come from memorizing answers.
They come from doing.
The Shift
This is the most important sentence.
SPIKE is not designed to teach you more theories.
Pause.
It is designed to help you become someone who creates value.
Define Entrepreneurship
When people hear the word entrepreneur...
they immediately think...
owning a company.
Pause.
That's only part of it.
Continue.
Entrepreneurship is really the ability to solve problems...
create opportunities...
lead people...
and build something that did not exist before.
Connect to AIA
That is exactly what our best financial entrepreneurs do.
They don't sell products.
Pause.
They build trust.
They build relationships.
They build teams.
They build businesses.
They build better futures.
Connect to SPIKE
During SPIKE...
you're not completing assignments just to receive a score.
Smile.
You're building a venture.
Start listing.
You'll create your ambition.
You'll define your purpose.
You'll build your Venture Blueprint.
You'll create your Dream Board.
You'll develop your Financial Entrepreneurship Canvas.
You'll conduct real market research.
You'll meet real clients.
You'll recruit teammates.
You'll build an agency.
Pause.
Everything you build becomes yours.
The Identity Shift
Walk toward the audience.
Speak slowly.
So today...
I want you to stop thinking of yourself...
Pause.
as an intern.
Pause.
Or even as a student.
Pause.
Start thinking of yourself...
Smile.
as a founder.
The Challenge
Founders don't wait for opportunities.
Pause.
They create them.
The Promise
By the time you finish SPIKE...
I don't simply want you to have completed 600 internship hours.
Pause.
I want you to have built something you're genuinely proud of.
Transition to the Portal
Smile.
Everything we've talked about today...
starts with one decision.
Pause.
Deciding to build.
Click.
Next slide.
Presentation Tips (Very Important)
Don't stand behind the podium.
Walk.
Use the stage.
This slide is about movement.
Your movement reinforces the idea of crossing the bridge.
Use silence.
The pauses are just as important as the words.
When you say:
SPIKE builds entrepreneurs.
Pause.
Let it sink in.
Ask questions.
Avoid long monologues.
Invite reflection.
The audience becomes mentally involved.
Speak from experience.
Share a personal example:
"When I graduated, I knew a lot about engineering. But I didn't yet know how to build a business, lead a team, or acquire clients. Those were skills I learned through experience—and that's exactly what SPIKE is designed to accelerate."
Stories make the message believable

Opening
Walk toward the audience.
Pause.
Smile.
Ask the room.
When you hear the word "internship"... what comes to mind?
Wait.
Write their answers if using a whiteboard.
Typical responses:
OJT
Office work
Photocopying
Encoding
Coffee
Assisting
Learning
Following instructions
Smile.
Those are all valid answers.
Pause.
The Reality
For many students...
an internship is simply about completing the required number of hours.
Pause.
Show up.
Finish the work.
Get evaluated.
Receive the certificate.
Pause.
Then move on.
The Shift
Point to the slide.
SPIKE certainly includes all of those things.
Pause.
But that's not where it ends.
Click.
Reveal:
This is where you'll build a business.
Pause.
The Big Idea
During the next several weeks...
you won't just complete assigned tasks.
Pause.
You'll create something that belongs to you.
Begin listing slowly.
Your own ambition.
Pause.
Your own purpose.
Pause.
Your own mission.
Pause.
Your own venture.
Pause.
Your own client portfolio.
Pause.
Your own business model.
Pause.
Your own professional brand.
Connect to Their Future
Think about graduation.
Pause.
Many graduates leave school with a diploma.
Pause.
That's valuable.
Pause.
But imagine graduating with...
Walk slowly.
A completed Venture Blueprint.
A professional portfolio.
Real client experience.
Research you've conducted yourself.
A growing professional network.
Leadership experience.
A business you can continue building.
Pause.
Which graduate do you think employers—and even clients—will remember?
Change the Identity
Walk closer.
Speak slowly.
I want you to stop asking...
Pause.
"What work will they give me?"
Pause.
Instead ask...
"What can I build?"
Pause.
That's how entrepreneurs think.
Connect to AIA
The most successful Agency Directors didn't become successful because someone gave them work.
Pause.
They built relationships.
They built trust.
They built teams.
They built opportunities.
They built businesses.
Pause.
That's exactly the mindset we'll begin developing here.
Introduce the Venture Portfolio
Every activity you complete in SPIKE...
Point toward the imaginary portal.
becomes part of your Venture Portfolio.
Not homework.
Not assignments.
Not paperwork.
Assets.
Everything you create has value.
List them.
Your Dream Board.
Your Financial Entrepreneurship Canvas.
Your Career Direction.
Your Research Findings.
Your Client Engagements.
Your Recruitment Journey.
Your Leadership Development.
Your Venture Blueprint.
Pause.
By Friday...
you'll already have the beginnings of something you can proudly present.
The Challenge
Walk toward the audience.
So I want to challenge you today.
Pause.
Don't participate just to finish your internship.
Pause.
Participate because you're building something that can continue long after your internship ends.
The Identity Shift
The most important sentence.
Speak slowly.
You're not here to complete hours.
Pause.
You're here to create momentum.
Pause.
Transition
Smile.
The next question is...
Pause.
What exactly are you going to build?
Click.
Move to Slide 10.
Delivery Notes
Keep moving.
This slide is about momentum.
Don't stay behind the podium.
Use the audience.
Ask questions.
Get responses.
Create conversation.
Personal Story
I would add one authentic story here.
For example:
"When I was starting my career, I thought success meant getting hired by a good company. Over time, I realized that the people who created the greatest opportunities weren't just looking for jobs—they were building relationships, solving problems, and creating value. That's the mindset SPIKE is designed to help you develop from day one."

Everything you create in SPIKE...
Pause.
doesn't disappear after Friday.
Pause.
It becomes part of your professional identity.
Pause.
This portfolio grows with you...
through research...
through clients...
through leadership...
through recruitment...
through becoming an Agency Director.
Now the audience is imagining
their future
, not reading bullets.
